title: Elatostema samarense
intro: Elatostema samarense, also known as Samaren's Elatostema, is a type of plant species belonging to the family Urticaceae, commonly found in tropical regions. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating infections. The bioactive constituents of Elatostema samarense include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Elatostema samarense is often prepared into teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Elatostema samarense is a species within the genus Elatostema, which is classified as part of the family Urticaceae, commonly known as the nettle family. This family comprises a diverse range of flowering plants, many of which are tropical in nature. Elatostema samarense, along with other members of the Urticaceae family, shares characteristics such as heart-shaped leaves and small, greenish or white flowers. The Elatostema genus, to which Elatostema samarense belongs, is primarily found in tropical regions of Asia and the Pacific Islands. Overall, the classification of Elatostema samarense reflects its unique characteristics and geographical distribution.
